summary: 25 years of kite design distilled into one kite. The Thrive is Ralf Groesel's all-rounder — 3-strut, 7-point fixed bridle, PRoWELD LE, Twincross Flylite canopy, Future Trailing Edge construction. Lightest sustainably-made high-performance kite on the market. 'Made for 90% of riders out there.' Massive size range 5–17m.
best_for: The one-kite quiver for serious all-around riding

sources.summary: Ralf Groesel built the kite he always wanted to build and it shows. The Thrive does everything well — jumps, waves, freeride — without feeling like a compromise. Best new all-rounder in years.
Harlem came out of nowhere and the Thrive is legit. Aaron Hadlow's involvement isn't just marketing — you feel his DNA in the kite's handling. Strong all-rounder.

reviews.video_title: Big Air Kite Review 12 - Harlem Thrive & Lead Bar

reviews.verdict: bably even over the force would be the 6 meter kite I'd be going for I'd have to wager that these Harlem kites are currently the top Kites on the market right now for the new school doubles and SL Loop variations so there you go another amazing kite uh that if you have the opportunity you should definitely give a try um as always like comment and subscribe and if you have a moment I would love to hear your thoughts on these Kites and any more in the comments below until next time goodbye [Music]
reviews.full_transcript: hello and welcome to another plus-sized kite review this time around my main focus in this comparison will be on the new Harlem Thrive but as always I will try my best to compare it to some of the other top Big Air kites currently on the market if you are new to this Channel and my videos you might want to do a little bit of homework first and check out my other reviews on the EVO DB Harlem force and perhaps some of the recent alula kites like the o Zone Vortex or slingshot NXT as I tend to reference my old reviews a lot if you find this video interesting entertaining at all useful or even just enjoy the lovely scenery from down here in New Zealand it would be most appreciated if you could drop a like And subscribe and be sure to throw in the comments any experiences you've had riding some of these kites I've reviewed recently or others you're hoping to see me review next you can also keep up with my upcoming kite reviews by following my Instagram or if you want to hear me talk about my review process check out the recent edition of The Kite surf 365 podcast that I did with my friend Adrian the host where we talk about my reviews woo worlds and what it's like kiing in New Zealand now getting back to the Harlem kite here as you are all probably aware this exciting new model was designed in collaboration with Ralph grel and manufactured at his industry-leading brainchild Factory in Macedonia Harlem were kind enough to get me a couple of sizes to demo for this review uh a 9 and 12 M and they also included their very well-designed new control bar so of course I'll give you my thoughts on that as well I don't believe there are any yet available on the Mac kiteboarding site but I put a link to the Harlem force in in the description and I believe they will have some in stock soon I have also included links to the Harlem page and to brainchild website in the description should you want to know more about the kite's technical aspects um and some of the fascinating technology involved in its creation and design I'll pretty much skip over talking all about that myself because there is plenty of information about it already out there so as usual I'll just focus on how the K feels performs and my experiences rioting it with each session uh now talking about the kiting because our season has been a bit shite so far it took me a couple weeks to actually get out on the 9M version so my first couple of Impressions rid in this kite were by using the 12 M size so I'll start there as you might expect like the Harlem Force when you first get the kite out of the bag you're not only impressed by its fairly flashy color scheme um but it's super lightweight just like an alula kite when you pump it up you better get something on top of it fairly quickly or risk it Sliding Away in a gust although it did seem to stay in place a little bit better than my dlabs um although they're the same weight I believe perhaps because the Leaning Edge material is not quite as stiff and slippery as alula this is not something I was really paying super attention to um just more of a cas ual observation I also found this material was a bit easier to pack away after my sessions which I really appreciated as it folds up uh much easier and cleaner than my Lula kites do uh but before we get to the Kites and detail I'll just quickly cover my feelings on the bar the design as a whole is really nice and clean and practical something I also appreciate about my North Navigator proar if you've watched that review looking over it it doesn't seem to have the same level of unique design as some of the bars I mentioned in my bar review uh like the sensor 3 Pro nav Pro or my trust bars and by that I mean it has certain areas like the pules and quick release that seem to follow a fairly generic design that you see with a lot of Industry bars um that are more standard in their parts but it does have more unique Design Elements around the grip and and the auto unwind in particular the grip itself is great in the hand rounded around the back through the base of your hand with a little elongated section in the front that would go under the base of your fingers to add a slightly more ergonomic feel than a standard fully rounded bar in the lighter winds and warmer sessions I really enjoyed the grip overall however it is a bit more firm than what I'm used to with my nav proar so this actually did bother me a little bit in the really strong cold recession I had the centerpiece or mono block as they call it is a single rectangular piece that flows through a very robust stainless steel centerpiece which I may prefer uh to the double tubes versions you get with other bars uh like my nav Pro or my core bar mainly because it ends up being slightly less wide and I don't have this weird fear the back of my head that I'm accidentally going to jam a finger through it the whole system works really smoothly um of course it is new but um I do always wonder how with auto unwind bars they will last and stand the test of time um I think I mentioned in my bar review I used to have a click bar years back with a single with a similar rectangular monoblock that would start to wear and cause it to bind up a little bit after a few months but noticing this one it has a larger gap for the block to roll through and two little circular steel pieces that seem to roll nicely and I would have to imagine this creates a lot less friction and must reduce the amount of sand in debris that would get stuck on it so I have High Hopes uh this one will last for quite some time the weight of the bar is a little heavier than my nav proar but not so much that it was an obvious distraction while kiting because uh actually had to pick them up when I was off the water to make sure I was even right about that um I would appear the added weight went into pieces like the stainless steel centerpiece which is something that admittedly I don't like the feel of when it was really cold but it does seem like a worthwhile upgrade because I really do hate having to replace the little plastic center pieces on my trust and sensor uh bars every 4 to six months those replacement pieces are quite cheap they're just super annoying to have to fit every time you replace them the lines are highquality sk99 plus lines so you know they will be durable but one thing I noticed is that where the Y splitter is there is no sleeving uh but instead what they've done is used a slightly thicker line up until the point of the splitter and the splitter is just attached directly onto that line this seems like a logical move move uh to solve the potential wear the Y splitter would cause but I would still recommend keeping an eye on that area and perhaps move it around every half dozen sessions or so to avoid anywhere at a single point so all in all this is a great bar and perhaps an ideal option for those highy Riders looking for a simpler more robust bar option with heavy duty lines that still has a smooth Auto unwind feature I think because of the slightly softer friendlier grip I would still prefer my nav Pro for low V riding kites but this one would potentially be my new go-to choice for high Y kites I'd have to say that although I do still prefer the lighter weight of the trust bar I just don't like having to replace its parts as often as you do or getting uh custom lines like I had to with mine uh so now that the bar is aside let's go down to the more serious interesting stuff the kites themselves and we'll get back into my first sessions on the 12 M size launching the kite you're immediately impressed with how light it feels not only on the bar but moving it around the window the kite is wildly fast and requires very little effort to fly it I was riding the 12 M with 24 M lines mainly which I found suited this larger size better I did also try sessions on 22 M lines but it gave up a bit of low end and just overall power so I would certainly suggest sticking with 24 M lines in the larger size kites if you can this whole session was only about 15 to 20 knots in the first one and I was able to consistently ride up wind and my standard board jump from around 5 to 12 m in the Gus and even snuck in a few Loops when I would uh get some stronger wind flow through nothing more than about 5 to 8 M though um for the loops it was very impressive how quickly the kite got around given it was a 12 M and as you can see here in this clip uh keep a nice high uh tight angle allowing me to do the loops fairly easily and get a little catch on them the clips of these Loops were in the stronger parts of the session though and what I would estimate was only about 18 to 20 knots uh in this first session I did swap over over to my 12 M Evo lab after about an hour and felt like I noticed it being a little bit heavier on the bar um but certainly within the margin for error uh I would say in the on andof wind we were getting um it's a bit hard to judge these things like bar pressure um but with that added uh bar pressure I also feel like the kite had more power um and when I went out and started jumping on the EVO I was getting a a little bit higher particularly um in the gusts when I tested the 12's again in future sessions I did find the same thing I would say at least in this size um after this first test the Evo did have a little bit better low end uh had a bit more potential power and was sort of better on the hang time um I noticed the Evo was a little more stable as well as the wind crept up with it not being as likely to Flap in the gust as you can see in this clip here where we're riding them together the Harlem did have a tendency to lose its shape as the wind got stronger at times but luckily never to an amount that seemed to impact its flight characteristics later on when I was able to ride the 9 M kites in over 35 knots I will talk more about uh these so one significant Advantage the thow I've had over my Evo in the 12 M side was that it seemed to Loop faster and the loops were friendlier so you could pull the bar in on a smaller jump and get a fairly easy High angled loop with very little pull now I wouldn't say you could Loop it in any less wind than my Evo because even though the Evo wanted to pull more and come a little lower in the window it does give you a little more lift and height to compensate I could tell already on the 12 that with these faster friendlier loops on the Thrive that it was going to be an ideal kite for progressing Loopers looking to send their first Loops especially if they have to use a larger kite size now a lot of people seem surprised when I say that I Loop my 12 but in reality most modern kites will Loop pretty well right up into those larger sizes you just need enough height wind and experience to get the kite around in the past I have looped 12 M orbits and even used to throw my old 2020 12 M pulse around I just needed enough wind on those kites to get at least 9 or so meters in the sky and admittedly you do feel your life flash before your eyes every time the kite would slowly move around and decide if it wanted to go back up above you so I certainly don't recommend it the nice thing about these two kites with the thrive in particular is you don't at all feel like you're going to have to take near as much risk especially when you can get away with looping at only 5 to six meters high now that I have a pretty good idea of what this kite was all about I was of course really excited to get the 9 meter in the air and I finally got the opportunity a little later in woo worlds on a team trip down to pton the spot that often gets some of the strongest albeit Gusty winds in the country luckily when we got there it was early enough in the morning before the wind was too nuclear so I was able to ride the N9 and I got to ride around on the 9 M Thrive for about an hour it was unfortunately very challenging wind as it always is there with big holes and big gusts but for the most part I always felt pretty secure on this kite even though it was my first time on it in this size I was able to Loop it literally my first jump as you can see in this one was around 10 m and I was boosting up to around 18 M on woo once again I did find this kite was losing its shape and some stability when the wind would gust over 30 35 knots pop I found it to be mainly when riding back and forth with the kite in a stationary position in the sky when you seem to engage the bar and direct the kite it stayed very responsive particularly through jumps and kite Loops it seemed to me like because it was only three struts it didn't have the overall rigidity and stability to fly perfectly in the wind like some of the top three strut alula kites would but the kite was still stiff enough and fast enough to overcome the lack of stability with its performance in a future session I did get a cleaner 35 to 40 knots on this nine and although I was still finding this was happening um I never felt like I was losing control of the kitee while straight jumping or looping yet again the shaking of the kite did keep me a bit nervous I have to admit though um so I think it would take something to get used to and I do have to wonder if it's more or less noticeable in the smaller sizes I rode this 9 M as I said up to around 40 knots um in the last session I had on it I was jumping over 20 M um and these were in winds that I could have easily gone down to an 8 meter which I actually ended up doing on that day um to compare it to a couple of other kites as you can see in this Drone footage here in Mir with the idea of what kind of conditions we had aside from these two nuclear sessions on the nine I also put it up in some really light winds for me with it um only around 20 to 25 knots where I had to ride my bigger 145 light wind board to make sure I could stay up wind and do some tricks on it I did this to get a good idea of the kite's low end and how it felt in tricks cuz we just haven't had a lot of 9M wind so like this front roll kite loop I was only 6 M High um and the kite did a great job as you can see uh of giving me just enough lift but also getting around super fast um I also lent this kite to a friend who's a regular Evo dlab Rider who's been struggling to progress her kite Loops um because I was really curious what she would think of it like me she found the kite a lot more forgiving to Loop and it actually helped to give her a bit of added confidence to go for them more often so how do I think it Compares overall in the key areas that I like to discuss starting with boosting and hangtime I think it is certainly one of the better ones I've tried if you can get the timing just right I was finding that I would over send the kite Affair a bit with how fast it is so didn't quite get the sweet spot as often as I might on say a rebel XR um but it was not as hard to find as I uh did originally with my dlabs that said it just didn't seem to as often reward you with some bonus lift like I find the top alula boosters like my DB or the slingshot NXT seam to so I would put its potential for boosting just behind that top tier of kites I think it has slightly more power and lift than say the top orbits the Nitro V2 or the force Landing it somewhere around the vortex and era area uh for potential although fellow flying kiwi Hugo and Toby might have to say something about the ultimate potential for the a given their recent woo worlds domination I do think because of this kite speed a lot of people myself included are going to struggle to get insane height with it right out of the bag on the smaller sizes but as I found with my last 9M session the kite is so light and easy to use you can ride it in pretty maxed out wind and still feel reasonably in control the hang time I believe is also just a tier below the top few floaters out there some somewhere again perhaps around the vortex which I actually think is just above the era and those other dakron kites I mentioned except for perhaps the code V2 which still has a lot of the same feel coming down as its Superior relative the NXT as with all kites keep in mind the way you fly them and your own skill level can often mitigate a kite's advantages or disadvantages to some extent but I am trying to give my opinion on what type of results most Riders would like get from these kites I was finding my Hang Time was being a little compromised by the Thrive due to how fast it is like with the force because it has a tendency to also hel Loop super fast if you didn't purposely send it a little wider above you you'd often risk not getting quite a soft Landing as a slower more naturally floaty kite this is something getting fairly common I found with the super fast looping kites these days now onto the loops this is where the kite really shines and like its close relative the Harlem force it is without a doubt one of the fastest looping Kites on the market I might put it just behind the force for its ability to Pivot around in a super tight Circle but it wouldn't be beat by very much I can see why Harlem team writer Lorenzo casati is really focusing his competition writing on double loops and other kite variations with this kite because it seems to offer you options nothing else on the market is currently capable of in regards to kite acrobatics as I mentioned earlier when describing the first session on the 12 whatever size you seem to be on the kite can Loop super high and tight if you just crank that bar and with very little pull in the loop which until very recently has been fairly uncommon with a more bow-shaped kite like this with the right timing you can get a similar effect on the EVO dlab but they still seem to have more yank to them in my opinion there are a few kites that I can think of with their shape now that would come close to looping so fast and tight U particularly the ozone Vortex and the core Pace being the first that come to mind the core being one which I've only been recently testing for another review so hopefully I'll be able to talk a lot more about that in a future video When comparing it to my favorite dakron Loop progression kites like the Nitros and orbits it shares some of those ideal characteristics of fast and tight with a more forgiving and less aggressive yank through the loop which make them all ideal for confidence building when it comes to Loop progression with this Thrive the speed is probably even faster than my or Pros or Nitros so it's certainly going to take some getting used to in the smaller sizes and I'm thinking might just be the best option for Loops in the 10 and up kite size uh I also tend to def finded with sizes 10 and up the increased speed seems to be something that benefits more Riders at least at an intermediate level and up as the increased size tends to make it easy enough to adapt to quickly comparing it to other dakron kites like the orbits or Nitros does seem a bit unfair of course uh on the whole because overall it seems to fall somewhere closer in performance and price I believe to an ulite although maybe being a tier below the price tag aside from Price the main advantage of these other dakron kites would have to be uh that they are five struts so I believe they do a little bit better job of holding their shape and their overall stability in the super strong conditions in the strongest session I had I actually went from my 9M Thrive down to the 8 m NXT before finally ending up on the 8 m Evo dlab and it felt like they all had about the same level of power in that 35 to 40 knot region with my jumping Heights across all three kites being within a meter or two all over 20 M it's a bit hard to say for sure but it did feel like the Thrive and Evo were probably looping a bit faster than the NXT and even though I was on a 9 M with the Thrive it still felt like it was staying a little higher in the window through the loops with less pull than those other 8 m kites it also felt uh noticeably lighter on the bar than the 8m NXT but heavier on the bar than my 8 m Evo I would have to imagine that if the wind did get any stronger in that session or I got any 50 plus knot squalls the instability would certainly start to become a big problem for me um and I would have lost all my confidence potentially to send it big on a nine whereas the other two kites being smaller sizes and having stiffer Luda frames handled the conditions better and of course kept my confidence a bit higher to just send it when it was absolutely nuking so finally moving on to the confidence building Factor as a whole for this kite well as I have touched on for almost anyone of an intermediate level and up I would say you could just jump on a size 10 in the Harlem and feel pretty dialed very quickly um because despite the kite's speed it just seems to have a fairly effortless nature particularly in the larger sizes so I think those would be a great Pace to start everyone certainly of that level could get used to the smaller sizes um it would just take a little bit more time on the water I think what um it may lack in boosting potential versus the top kites in that area it does certainly make up for in its overall ease of use so this could be really one of the better kites out there for Riders wanting to progress their big air um particularly into kite Loops um but who are also still looking to you know build on their jumping ability in general for me personally I'm not quite sure it would be enough to get me away from my daab quiver just yet given the investment I already put into them last year but if I was not already struggling to get through all the nines I have to review um and was just looking to have a 9 meter of my own this would be certainly one of the few that I would actually consider getting to go along with my DB quiver because I think I would find it one of the easier kites to progress with kite Loop variations on um I should also add if I was ever looking to get into doubles this um probably even over the force would be the 6 meter kite I'd be going for I'd have to wager that these Harlem kites are currently the top Kites on the market right now for the new school doubles and SL Loop variations so there you go another amazing kite uh that if you have the opportunity you should definitely give a try um as always like comment and subscribe and if you have a moment I would love to hear your thoughts on these Kites and any more in the comments below until next time goodbye [Music]
